name: Philosophical / methodological objection

## The commitment you take to be unsound

<!-- One of the six commitments in the Method page, or a more general claim. Be precise about which. -->

## Your reading of the paper passage the commitment rests on

<!-- Cite the section and quote the relevant passage. The paper is in research/truth-ethics-benefit-preprint.md. -->

## Counterexample, alternative reading, or steel-man challenge

<!-- The strongest version of the objection. -->

## What should change in the application as a consequence

<!-- A specific, actionable change: this scoring step should be different / this output class should be added / this disclosure should be reworded / etc. -->

## Notes

<!-- Anything else: prior literature on this objection, related debates in §6 of the paper, how the framework handles adjacent cases. -->
